name: Google Sheets
description: Read, write, create, and SHARE Google Sheets from this agent. Install it, then click Connect Google on the agent's Deployment tab to authorize an account, and the agent gets google_sheets__* tools (read/append/update cells, metadata, batch updates, create spreadsheets) plus google_drive__* tools (share the sheets it creates with people or by link) via eve's OpenAPI connections — access tokens self-refresh at runtime. Connect a dedicated Google account and the agent effectively has its own sheets: with the drive.file scope it can only reach the spreadsheets it created, so it owns and shares exactly what it makes.

secrets.name: GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID
secrets.description: OAuth client id of Eden's shared Google app — provisioned automatically at deploy from the agent's Google connection. Used to refresh access tokens.

secrets.name: GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET
secrets.description: OAuth client secret of Eden's shared Google app — provisioned automatically at deploy. Needed for the refresh-token grant.

secrets.name: GOOGLE_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN
secrets.description: The agent's Google refresh token, captured when you click Connect Google on the Deployment tab — provisioned automatically at deploy (sealed in Eden's database until then).

secrets.name: GOOGLE_OAUTH_SCOPES
secrets.description: The scopes the connected account actually granted, space-joined — provisioned automatically at deploy so the agent can tell whether the drive.file sharing permission is present before attempting to share.

setup:
**Connect (recommended):** after installing, open the agent's **Deployment** tab and click **Connect Google** on the Connections card. Approve one consent screen — the `spreadsheets` scope (read/write/create) and the `drive.file` scope (share the sheets the agent creates) — and Eden stores the grant; nothing to copy. The agent then has `google_sheets__*` tools for reading, writing, and creating spreadsheets, plus `google_drive__*` tools for sharing them.

**Give the agent its own sheets:** connect a **dedicated Google account** (a fresh Gmail made for this agent) rather than your personal one. With the `drive.file` scope the agent can only see the spreadsheets it created, so everything it makes lives in — and is shared from — that account's own Drive, cleanly separated from your files. (Connecting your own account works too; then new sheets land in your Drive.)

**Self-host note (operators):** configure ONE Google OAuth client for this Eden installation before the Connect button works.

1. In **Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services**, enable BOTH the **Google Sheets API** and the **Google Drive API** (Drive powers sharing).
2. Under **Credentials**, create an **OAuth client ID** of type **Web application** (or reuse the one from another Google connector — one client serves every Google connection).
3. Add `<origin>/google/callback` to its **Authorized redirect URIs** (e.g. `https://eden.example.com/google/callback`, and `http://localhost:5173/google/callback` for local dev).
4. Set `EDEN_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` and `EDEN_GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` on the control plane.

**Publishing status matters** — it controls verification and refresh-token lifetime. Both `spreadsheets` and `drive.file` are **sensitive** scopes (`drive.file` is the narrowest Drive scope — access only to app-created files — but still sensitive):
- **Internal** (Google Workspace org only): no verification, refresh tokens do **not** expire. Best for a single organization.
- **External + Testing**: up to 100 test users, but refresh tokens **expire after 7 days**, so agents must be reconnected weekly. Fine for evaluation.
- **External + Production**: refresh tokens are durable, but the sensitive scopes require Google app verification (brand review, possibly a security assessment) before general users can consent.

Pick **Internal** if you self-host for one Workspace org; plan for verification if you expose this to external users in Production.
